CTN 4, 095 [Muššu’u 5][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
(start of column missing)
o i 1'o 1'

[... e₂-kur]-ra-[ke₄]

(o i 1') ... of the Ekur!

x x [... E₂].KUR
o i 2'o 2'

dama-šu-ḫal-⸢bi ama⸣ niŋ₂-⸢zi-ŋal₂⸣-la-ke₄

(o i 2') Amašuhalbi, mother of living things!

dAMA.ŠU.ḪAL.⸢BI⸣ um-⸢mi⸣ [šik]-⸢na⸣-at ZI⸢tu₄⸣
o i 3'o 3'

[dtu₆]-en₂-bi-e-ne-en₂-na-⸢sag₉⸣-[ga] x x x [x x] x

(o i 3') Tuenbieneenasaga, glorious minister of the Egalmah!

d⸢MIN⸣<(TU₆.EN₂.BI.E.NE.EN₂.NA.SA₆.GA)> ⸢SUKKAL ṣi-ru⸣ ša₂ qe₂-reb [E₂.GAL].MAḪ
o i 4'o 4'

⸢saŋ⸣-gig zu₂-⸢gig⸣ šag₄-gig [x x]

(o i 4') May she (Ninisina) tear out with her great hands (Akkadian: hands which are very great) headache, toothache, heartache, heart constriction, eye disease, asag-disease, samana-disease, evil illness, heat stroke, gust of wind disease, the cause of anything evil.

[mu-ru]-uṣ qaq-qa-di mu-ru-uṣ [x x] ⸢mu⸣-ru-⸢uṣ⸣ [x x] ⸢ki-is ŠA₃⸣
o i 5'o 5'

⸢igi⸣-gig a₂-sag₃ sa-ma-⸢na₂ tur₅-ra ḫulu⸣-ŋal₂

mu-ru-uṣ i-ni a-⸢sak-ku sa⸣-ma-⸢nu mur⸣-[ṣu] ⸢lem-nu⸣
o i 6'o 6'

⸢ud-da tab⸣-ba tumu-dal-a ⸢niŋ₂-nam ḫul⸣ dim₂-ma-a-[...]

⸢ḫi-miṭ⸣ ṣe-e-tu ši-⸢biṭ⸣ [ša₂]-a-<ri> mim-ma ⸢e?⸣-[piš? x x]
o i 7'o 7'

⸢šu⸣-ne-ne gal?-a-ni ḫa-⸢ba-ra-ab-zi⸣-[zi]

ina ŠU-MIN ša₂ rab?-ba-a-ti ⸢li-is⸣-[suḫ-šu₂]
o i 8'o 8'

⸢šu sed⸣-da-a-ni ḫe₂-em-x-x-⸢ur₃?⸣

(o i 8') May she erase them with her soothing hands (Akkadian: hands which are soothing).

ina [ŠU]-⸢MIN⸣-šu₂ ša₂ pa-aš₂-ḫa-ti ⸢li⸣-maš-⸢šid⸣-[su]
o i 9'o 9'

inim den-ki-ke₄ pa e₃-[a]

(o i 9') Make visible the command of Enki (Akkadian: May the command of Ea be visible).

a-mat de₂-a liš-te-[pi]
o i 10'o 10'

ddam-gal-nun-na ḫe₂-en-si-sa₂-⸢e⸣

(o i 10') May Damgalnuna (Akkadian: Damkina) bring order.

dDAM.KI.NA liš-te-[šir]
o i 11'o 11'

d⸢asar⸣-alim-nun-na dumu-saŋ ⸢abzu⸣-[ke₄ ...]

(o i 11') Asaralimnuna (Akkadian: Marduk), first-born of the Abzu, [...] (and) making better are yours. ....

dAMAR.UTU DUMU reš-⸢tu⸣-u₂ ša₂ ap-⸢si⸣-[i ...]  ; du-mu-qu ku-[um-mu ...]

(o i 12') Incantation: ... of heaven, ... of Ea, the sage of ...

(r i 18') [...] the one of his god and his goddess.

ša₂ DINGIR IRI-šu u d15 ⸢IRI⸣-[šu x x]

šar₅ 1 ⸢DANNA⸣ ina ⸢SU-šu₂⸣ [...]

u₂-suḫ qa-⸢as⸣-[su ...]

Column ii
(start of column missing)
r ii 1'r 1'

[...] x

(r ii 1') [...] ....

r ii 2'2'

[x x me-ḫe]-⸢e⸣ šu-[te]-⸢ši⸣-ru

(r ii 2') [...] the storm wind bring order.

r ii 3'3'

[...] ⸢ta⸣-a-⸢ra-ta⸣

(r ii 3') [...] you are turned back.

r ii 4'4'

[... tu₁₅KUR?].⸢RA?⸣ tu₁₅MAR.TU

(r ii 4') Let the [...], the east wind (and) the west wind, the four [...], blow.

r ii 5'5'

[x er]-⸢be₂-tu li⸣-zi-qu₂-⸢nim⸣-ma

r ii 6'6'

⸢at⸣-ta mim-ma lem-nu ina zu-mur NENNI ⸢A⸣ NENNI

(r ii 6') Whatever evil you are in the body of so-and-so, son of so-and-so, you should not sigh, you should not blow, you should not ....

r ii 7'7'

a-i ta-ḫa-a-si ⸢i ta⸣-a-zi-qa a ta-x-ḪAR

r ii 8'8'

⸢ḫul⸣-dub₂ zi an ⸢ḫe₂-pad₃⸣ zi ki ḫe₂-pad₃

(r ii 8') May the huldub-demon be adjured by the life of heaven, may it be adjured by the life of the underworld.

r ii 9'9'

[x ḫul]-⸢ŋal₂⸣ [bar]-še₃ ḫe₂-em-ta-gub

(r ii 9') May the evil [...] stand to the side.

r ii 10'10'

[... DINGIR]-⸢šu ša₂ ABGAL DINGIR-MEŠ⸣ [...]

(r ii 10') [...] of his god, of the sage of the gods, [...].

r ii 11'11'

[...] ⸢d⸣AMAR.UTU mim-ma ina SU-ka ⸢li⸣-[is-suḫ]

(r ii 11') May [...], Marduk, tear out whatever is in your body.

r ii 12'12'

[...] ⸢d⸣NAM.TAR-ka dASAR.LU₂.ḪI lik!(AB₂-)⸢kis⸣

(r ii 12') May Asalluhi cut off your fate [...].

r ii 13'13'

[...] ⸢dNAM⸣.TAR-ka dASAR.LU₂.ḪI lid-di-ka

(r ii 13') May Asalluhi throw your fate [...] for you.

r ii 14'14'

[x] x [x d]⸢NAM⸣.TAR-ka dASAR.LU₂.ḪI lig-mil-ka

(r ii 14') May Asalluhi save you [...] ... [...] of your fate.

r ii 15'15'

⸢ši⸣-pat-[su] ⸢ša?⸣ TI.LA dAMAR.UTU lid-di-ka

(r ii 15') May Marduk recite his incantation of life over you.

r ii 16'16'

⸢TU₁₅⸣ DU₁₀.GA ⸢ša⸣ dEN.BI.LU.LU li-zi-qa-ka

(r ii 16') May the good breath of Enbilulu blow over you.

r ii 17'17'

dŠA₃.ZU ina dan-na-at ŠU-MIN-ka li-iṣ-bat

(r ii 17') May Šazu seize your hands in (a time of) difficulty.

r ii 18'18'

⸢ti-bi⸣-ma ⸢bu-luṭ⸣ ša₂ dAMAR.UTU at-ta EN₂

(r ii 18') Arise and recover, you are the one of Marduk. Incantation.

r ii 19'19'

⸢EN₂ uš₂⸣ ḫul-⸢ŋal₂⸣ an ki-bi-da

(r ii 19') Incantation: Evil death, heaven and the underworld.

(rest of column missing)
